title: Misleading error message when trying to load image from compressed archive when disk space is too low on macOS
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

Unsure about the label as bug or feature request. Need guidance for the right label, sorry!

**Description**

When attempting to load an image from a compressed archive on macOS and the VMs disk size is not sufficient to do so, the error message is misleading.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

`podman load -i some-image-that-is-too-big.tar.gz`


**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: unable to load image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)`

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected a more useful error message, something like:

`Error: unable to load image: disk size of VM not sufficient to load given image. Try creating a new image with an extended disk size using podman machine init NEW_MACHINE --disk-size 30`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec  8 19:41:11 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 22:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

  /usr/local/share/fish/vendor_completions.d
==> Analytics

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, quickly looked into the troubleshooting site and didn't find anything yet. Maybe this could be added there. The steps that resolved the issue for me were:

1. create new VM with more disk space `podman machine init MACHINE_NAME --disk-size 20` (for 20GB of VM disk size)
2. stop the default VM `podman machine stop podman-machine-default` (you can list all machines with `podman machine list`, the asterix ("*") seems to indicate the default connection)
3. start the new VM: `podman machine start MACHINE_NAME`
4. switch the default connection to the newly created VM with `podman system connection default MACHINE_NAME`
5. try loading the image again, should work now

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

On macOS 12.1 (21C52), Intel Mac.

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out.  The error reporting has been improved in the meantime and will be released with Podman 4.0.
Oh perfect, that's great news! Looking forward to version 4 then :)
